excerpt: It is incumbent upon you to enter into obedience to God through the covenant (ʿahd) that has been enjoined upon you; through this covenant you may enter the sanctuary of the Imam...for there exists no path to salvation save through him.
source_location_hint: Early Ismaili ethics and covenant literature; preserved in KASHKOLE

excerpt: In your dealings and transactions with whomever you encounter, you shall speak with excellence, truthfulness, loyalty, and goodness. These are all the attributes of piety (taqwā), and they are the finest treasures of the Hereafter.
source_location_hint: Section on ethical conduct and obedience

excerpt: This covenant is the trust of God, His Messenger, and the Imam of the Age. Whoever betrays it shall suffer grievous harm in both religion and worldly life. He shall go astray; the wrath of Allah shall descend upon him.

Because you have sought Our eternity by acknowledging your infirmity, We bestow upon you the garment of eternal life. Because you have sought Our pleasure by expressing your helplessness, We grant you wealth that shall never diminish...We give you the garment of obedience...We have blessed you with the garment of forgiveness.
source_work: The State of an Unbridled Wayfarer parable; traditional Ismaili spiritual narrative
source_authority: KASHKOLE, Binder 27, Chapter 17; compiled by Ḥāj Mullā Aḥmad Khurshīd Ḥasan
source_location_hint: Sufi-influenced parable illustrating divine mercy and human repentance

excerpt: When the Messenger of Allah said 'Gabriel has come to me,' Khadīja should place her hand upon his naked thigh and ask if he still saw Gabriel. If Gabriel were present, he would say yes; but when she uncovered her hair, Gabriel would depart, for the hair of a woman is part of her private parts.
source_work: Confirmation of the Prophet's Revelation; hadith-based narrative on the testing of the revelation
source_location_hint: Surah tradition regarding Khadīja and the verification of revelation

excerpt: After performing tasbīḥ (glorification), prostration, and worship, there arrives a station where belief itself comes to the worshipper—he no longer needs to go in search of belief. The perfection of prayer lies in its prostration, and when one raises one's head from the final prostration, one arrives at certainty (yaqīn).
source_work: Complete Certainty (Yaqīn Kāmil); Qur'ānic commentary tradition
source_authority: KASHKOLE, Binder 27, Chapter 17; Majlis of Mullā Ghulām Ḥusayn ibn Nasrullāh
source_location_hint: Based on Quran 15:98-99; Ismaili taʾwīl (esoteric exegesis) tradition

excerpt: Though Allah is the Pardoner and the Merciful, forgiving every sin, He shall never forgive the hypocrites. Hypocrisy is the devil that pervades our bodies. Among hypocrites, all three forms of evil converge, and they shall be punished in the lowest reaches of the worst valley of Hell.
source_work: The Hypocrite (Munāfiq) and the Apostate (Murtadd); Qur'ānic and traditional law commentary
source_location_hint: Based on Qur'ānic verses on hypocrisy (munāfiqūn); Ismaili legal and ethical interpretation

excerpt: At the conquest of Mecca, where the Prophet proclaimed general amnesty, he nonetheless named certain hypocrites and ordered that they be killed wherever found. The three caliphs had relations or old friendships with some of these hypocrites, yet dared not obey the Prophet's command, and not only released these hypocrites but some even sheltered them.
source_work: Ulu al-Akhbār, cited through Ismaili traditionist compilation
source_location_hint: Ismaili historical and legal tradition regarding caliphal authority and the Prophet's commands

excerpt: Huwayrath ibn Nafīl, with whom Mawlānā ʿAlī had once been acquainted, came before ʿAlī. The Imām said: 'O enemy of Allah, you are here!' The man pleaded, 'O my brother ʿAlī, do not kill me.' ʿAlī replied, 'May Allah not preserve you over me,' and slew him immediately.
source_work: Lessons of the Night of Hijrah (year 500 AH); hadith tradition on ʿAlī's execution of hypocrites
source_location_hint: Early Islamic hadith tradition on the killing of named hypocrites at the Prophet's command

excerpt: The time approaches, therefore, for the return of the Messenger of Allah—no longer in his initial dispensation, but in the form of Imam al-Mahdī (the Guided One) of the latter days.
source_location_hint: oral transmission within the Ismaili community

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah brought the outer, the zāhir (manifest); when he returns in the form of al-Mahdī, he shall reveal to all humanity the bātin (inner, esoteric) dimension of the manifest word.
source_work: al-Aqwal al-Dhahabiyya or cognate Ismaili doctrinal traditions
source_location_hint: doctrinal teachings on the two dimensions of revelation

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah declared: 'Though the mothers of all the Prophets differ, they are brothers to one another, and their religion is one. Jesus and I stand in particular closeness; no other prophet came between him and me.'
source_work: Nahj al-Balagha or cognate hadith collections
source_location_hint: teachings on the relationship between prophets and the imminent return of Jesus
